perturbation.original_text: The Professional agrees to perform the following services during the term of this Agreement: a. The Professional agrees that, with respect to men's apparel, he will exclusively endorse and use exclusively in play, practice, exhibits, clinics and other events open to the media or public, Haley brand apparel which shall consist of shirts, vests, jackets, sweaters, pants and shorts (if permitted by applicable rules).
perturbation.changed_text: The Professional agrees to perform the following services during the term of this Agreement: a. The Professional agrees that, with respect to men's apparel, he will exclusively endorse and use exclusively in play, practice, exhibits, clinics and other events open to the media or public, Haley brand apparel which shall consist of shirts, vests, jackets, sweaters, pants and shorts (if permitted by applicable guidelines).
perturbation.explanation: The term "rules" is replaced with "guidelines." While seemingly similar, "rules" implies a stricter, more binding obligation, while "guidelines" suggests a more flexible, advisory approach. This introduces ambiguity as to the Professional's obligation to adhere to apparel restrictions during events. This change creates a contradiction because in the original text implies a binding contract and the word guidelines suggests a suggestion and non-binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SERVICES OF THE PROFESSIONAL. The Professional agrees to perform the following services during the term of this Agreement: a. The Professional agrees that, with respect to men's apparel, he will exclusively endorse and use exclusively in play, practice, exhibits, clinics and other events open to the media or public, Haley brand apparel which shall consist of shirts, vests, jackets, sweaters, pants and shorts (if permitted by applicable guidelines).
perturbation.justification: While the change from 'rules' to 'guidelines' introduces a subtle shift in the obligation, it does not create a strong, undeniable contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text already uses 'guidelines'. A reasonable interpretation would be that the Professional must adhere to Haley brand apparel if permitted by applicable guidelines, as provided by the modified text, and the meaning remains consistent in context.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall have unlimited rights of utilization of the Professional's Image in all advertising, promotion, publicity and other forms of communication with any part during the term of this Agreement, it being the intent of the Professional that the Company's utilization of the Professional's Image shall be at the discretion of the Company. The right of usage described herein shall be subject to the requirement that the Company shall not place the Professional's Image in an unfavorable light.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall have unlimited rights of utilization of the Professional's Image in all advertising, promotion, publicity and other forms of communication with any entity during the term of this Agreement, it being the intent of the Professional that the Company's utilization of the Professional's Image shall be at the discretion of the Company. The right of usage described herein shall be subject to the requirement that the Company shall not place the Professional's Image in an unfavorable light.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "any part" to "any entity" introduces ambiguity and potential contradiction. While both terms broadly refer to external parties, "part" in the original context implies a broader scope that could even include internal divisions within the company. "Entity" typically refers to distinct legal or commercial organizations. This could lead to disputes over whether the usage rights extend to, for example, internal promotional materials that are not directly targeted at external 'entities'. This change introduce uncertainty regarding the scope of third parties to whom the company can communicate using the professional's image.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RIGHTS OF USAGE. As described in Section 1(b) above, the Professional has granted the company an exclusive worldwide right and license to use his name, autograph, likeness, photographs, electronic media depiction, signature and any other words, symbols or depiction's (hereinafter the "Professional's Image") which will identify the Professional to the public in connection with the advertising, promotion, publicizing, sale and distribution of Haley apparel. The Company shall have unlimited rights of utilization of the Professional's Image in all advertising, promotion, publicity and other forms of communication with any entity during the term of this Agreement, it being the intent of the Professional that the Company's utilization of the Professional's Image shall be at the discretion of the Company. The right of usage described herein shall be subject to the requirement that the Company shall not place the Professional's Image in an unfavorable light. The Professional or the Professional's designated agent shall have the right to review any advertising, promotion or publicity materials utilized by the company which contain the Professional's Image on reasonable written or oral request to the Company during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "any part" to "any entity" introduces ambiguity and potential contradiction. The original context of "part" implies a broader scope, possibly including internal divisions. "Entity" typically refers to distinct legal or commercial organizations. This introduces uncertainty regarding the scope of third parties to whom the company can communicate using the professional's image as presented in the 'RIGHTS OF USAGE' section in the 'Contradicted Text'. This change can be seen as Misaligned Terminology, which creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of any dispute which arises between the parties and which relates to the subject matter of this Agreement, the parties acknowledge and agree that any such dispute shall be submitted for binding arbitration in accordance with the arbitration procedures established by the American arbitration Association in Denver, Colorado. If such association is not then in existence, an independent association of arbitrators may be utilized which is designated by agreement of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of any disagreement which arises between the parties and which relates to the subject matter of this Agreement, the parties acknowledge and agree that any such disagreement shall be submitted for binding arbitration in accordance with the arbitration procedures established by the American arbitration Association in Denver, Colorado. If such association is not then in existence, an independent association of arbitrators may be utilized which is designated by agreement of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The word "dispute" is replaced with "disagreement." While these terms are often used synonymously, 'dispute' typically implies a more formal and significant conflict, while 'disagreement' can refer to minor differences of opinion. This change creates uncertainty about what level of conflict triggers the binding arbitration clause. If a minor disagreement arises, is it still subject to binding arbitration, or is that reserved for more serious 'disputes'?
perturbation.contradicted_text: DISPUTES. In the event of any disagreement which arises between the parties and which relates to the subject matter of this Agreement, the parties acknowledge and agree that any such disagreement shall be submitted for binding arbitration in accordance with the arbitration procedures established by the American arbitration Association in Denver, Colorado. If such association is not then in existence, an independent association of arbitrators may be utilized which is designated by agreement of the parties. In the event the parties are unable to agree on an independent association of arbitrators, either party may apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for appointment of arbitrators.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'dispute' with 'disagreement.' While the explanation suggests this introduces ambiguity, the contradicted text also uses 'disagreement,' meaning the change doesn't create a direct contradiction. The meaning is consistent in both sections. Therefore, there is no strong in-text contradiction.
